kicker: Software engineer · part-time creative artist & educator — most people online know me as Khesir.
bio: I build software, and I make sure I can stand behind every part of it. Working at an agentic level with AI, backed by real judgment, is the part of engineering I care about most.

I'm comfortable working at an **agentic level**, using AI to move faster on repetitive work. I take full ownership of everything I ship: I can explain the *reasoning* behind every decision, not just that it works.

I was always the creative type as a kid, but drawn more to *systems* than pure improvisation, figuring out how to make things efficient. That instinct sharpened when I found web development. The deeper I got into software, the more I loved the power to build things **entirely on my own terms**, especially after watching software get worse around me, from ad-free games to forced ads and subscriptions. Every project since has come from that same drive.

I want to lean further into **agentic AI** for the specialized, explicit parts of engineering, like frontend work, to free up time for something else I care about. After five years of coding taking up **90%** of my time, I'm shifting that balance toward *technical art*: bringing my software background into 2D animation and illustration.

education.description: Focused on software engineering fundamentals — web development, systems, and data structures.

services.title: Character Art
services.description: Character concepts, portraits, and stylized illustration — from rough sketch to finished, polished art.

services.title: Technical Art
services.description: Building shaders, procedural tools, and pipelines that bring illustration and engineering together in real time.

services.title: Full-stack Dev
services.description: Designing and building scalable frontend and backend systems — focused on performance and clean architecture.
